title: quercus alba
intro: Quercus alba, also known as the white oak tree, is a species of deciduous tree belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The bark and leaves of Quercus alba have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus alba may include tea, capsules, or tinctures, often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
